vectors.name: Boolean Type Fundamentals
vectors.description: Turbo Pascal implements a strict Boolean type natively. In memory, it is an ordinal type where False evaluates to 0 and True evaluates to 1.

quirks_and_tests.description: Validates the native ordinal memory mapping of Boolean values.

vectors.name: Logical NOT Operator
vectors.description: Inverts the truth value of a Boolean expression.

quirks_and_tests.description: When applied to a Boolean type, NOT performs a pure logical negation.

vectors.name: Logical AND Operator
vectors.syntax: Operand1: Boolean AND Operand2: Boolean; { Returns Boolean }
vectors.description: Performs a logical conjunction on two Boolean expressions. By default, Turbo Pascal employs short-circuit evaluation ({$B-}).

quirks_and_tests.description: CRITICAL ARCHITECTURE: If the first operand of an AND is False, the second operand is skipped entirely. This test validates short-circuiting by attempting a division by zero using a variable (to bypass compile-time constant folding). If evaluated at runtime, it would trigger Runtime Error 200.
setup: IF (False) AND (10 DIV ZeroVarAnd = 1) THEN SurvivedAndShort := False ELSE SurvivedAndShort := True;

vectors.name: Logical OR Operator
vectors.syntax: Operand1: Boolean OR Operand2: Boolean; { Returns Boolean }
vectors.description: Performs a logical disjunction on two Boolean expressions. Also subject to short-circuit evaluation ({$B-}).

quirks_and_tests.description: CRITICAL ARCHITECTURE: If the first operand of an OR is True, the compiler knows the overall expression is True and skips the second operand.
setup: IF (True) OR (10 DIV ZeroVarOr = 1) THEN SurvivedOrShort := True ELSE SurvivedOrShort := False;

vectors.name: Logical XOR Operator
vectors.syntax: Operand1: Boolean XOR Operand2: Boolean; { Returns Boolean }
vectors.description: Performs a logical exclusive disjunction. Returns True if operands are strictly different.

quirks_and_tests.description: Unlike AND/OR, XOR cannot be short-circuited because both operands are always mathematically required to determine the final state.

vectors.name: Bitwise NOT Operator
vectors.description: Performs a bitwise one's complement operation on the binary representation of an ordinal integer type.

quirks_and_tests.description: Turbo Pascal overloads the NOT operator based on the operand type. When given an integer, it flips all bits (e.g., NOT 0 yields -1 in two's complement arithmetic).

vectors.name: Bitwise AND Operator
vectors.syntax: Operand1: Integer AND Operand2: Integer; { Returns Integer }
vectors.description: Performs a bitwise logical AND between the bits of two integers.

quirks_and_tests.description: Overloaded for integers. Evaluates strictly at the bit level without any short-circuiting mechanics. (170 is $AA, 85 is $55).

vectors.name: Bitwise OR Operator
vectors.syntax: Operand1: Integer OR Operand2: Integer; { Returns Integer }
vectors.description: Performs a bitwise logical OR between the bits of two integers.

quirks_and_tests.description: Combines the bits of two integer values.

vectors.name: Bitwise XOR Operator
vectors.syntax: Operand1: Integer XOR Operand2: Integer; { Returns Integer }
vectors.description: Performs a bitwise logical Exclusive OR between two integers.

quirks_and_tests.description: Toggles bits where the mask has a 1.

vectors.name: Bit Shift Left (SHL)
vectors.syntax: Value: Integer SHL Count: Byte; { Returns Integer }
vectors.description: Shifts the bits of an integer value to the left by the specified count, filling vacated bits with zeros.

quirks_and_tests.description: A low-level arithmetic shift, highly optimized by the compiler. Functionally equivalent to multiplying by 2^Count.

vectors.name: Bit Shift Right (SHR)
vectors.syntax: Value: Integer SHR Count: Byte; { Returns Integer }
vectors.description: Shifts the bits of an integer value to the right by the specified count, filling vacated bits with zeros.

quirks_and_tests.description: Logical right shift. Equivalent to an integer division by 2^Count.
